role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; co-subject of a joint oral history with Cynthia Anderson (catalog entry 25, publication-block tier).
ai_reading: Fletcher Anderson shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 25 (catalog slug 'cynthia-and-fletcher-anderson'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Fletcher Anderson is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 25 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Fletcher Anderson; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Independent biographical sourcing for Fletcher Anderson is pending.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 25
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: Robert Bob Hicks House, Bogalusa, Louisiana
gallery.caption: The Robert Bob Hicks House in Bogalusa, Louisiana, headquarters of the Deacons for Defense and Justice, the armed self-defense group that protected the Black community where Fletcher Anderson worked at the Crown Zellerbach mill and joined the movement.
